site.title: Nodehome
site.description: A running publication about local models, private inference, self-hosted agents, weird hardware, research sweeps, and the builders wiring their own AI stack together.
site.eyebrow: Independent publication // local-first AI // field reports from owned systems
site.editor_note: AI is getting physical again. It shows up in terminals, racks, side projects, and ugly little workflows people actually control.
site.about: Nodehome covers AI once it leaves the browser and hits real machines: local models, private inference, self-hosted agents, rack builds, research sweeps, and the people doing the wiring themselves.
site.signal: A link-forward publication about owned AI systems. Less launch theater, more model servers, thermals, quantization, note pipelines, and field notes from the local-first frontier.

posts.title: Weekly Node Sweep: First Signals from the Local-First Frontier
posts.excerpt: A weekly rollup of the strongest signals from the Nodehome sweep system: model releases, local inference infra, hardware notes, and where the local-first scene is actually moving.

posts.title: Karpathy Research Dossier
posts.excerpt: A structured dossier on Andrej Karpathy's current projects, vocabulary, workflows, and the ideas shaping the local AI builder ecosystem.

posts.title: RTX 3090 Blower Market Intel, April 2026
posts.excerpt: Real market pricing, scam patterns, seller behavior, and what actually matters when sourcing blower 3090s for dense local inference builds.

posts.title: AI Agent Traps for Local Research Systems
posts.excerpt: How trap categories from current agent research map onto local knowledge pipelines, autonomous sweeps, and builder workflows.

posts.title: GPU Architecture Comparison for Local AI Builders
posts.excerpt: A practical comparison of 3090s, 4090s, 5090s, A100s, and H100s through the lens of local inference, not data center marketing.

posts.title: Sovereign Node v1.0 Build and Configuration Guide
posts.excerpt: A real-world multi-GPU rack build guide covering BIOS settings, Linux/NVIDIA setup, multi-GPU inference, and the gotchas that actually matter.
